purpose: AI-powered task planner that decomposes a natural-language work request into a parallel task swarm structure (initialization.json shape: task, agents[], waves[], chains[], history[]). Builds a strict prompt with embedded JSON-only output rules, calls api.spawnWorker with the configured agentProvider (claude or codex) using a synthetic taskId='_planner', subscribes to worker-output (accumulating chunks into planOutputRef) and worker-complete events. extractJSON tries multiple parse strategies: direct regex, NDJSON line-by-line accumulating assistant.message.content text blocks (Claude SDK stream-json format), flat-format fallback, and result message — robust to multiple stream formats. Once parsed, renders PlanPreview (waves with nested agents and dependency arrows) and offers 'Edit Plan' or 'Accept & Launch' — both call onPlanReady(plan) so the parent can either open the SwarmBuilder or launch directly.
gotchas: Listener cleanup is fragile — outputListenerRef.current and completeListenerRef.current are unsubscribe functions returned by api.on(). They are nulled inside complete handler AND in unmount cleanup. If the user closes the modal mid-generation, complete may never fire, but unmount will still clean up — verify both paths work.

spawnWorker uses taskId='_planner' as a sentinel. All worker-output / worker-complete events are filtered by data.taskId !== '_planner'. This prevents cross-talk with real swarm events but means concurrent planning sessions on different dashboards cannot run simultaneously without taskId collision.
dangerouslySkipPermissions: true is hardcoded — the planner never asks permission. Acceptable because it only writes to its prompt, but worth noting if security policy changes.

extractJSON regex /\{[\s\S]*"task"[\s\S]*"agents"[\s\S]*\}/ is greedy — matches from FIRST { to LAST } containing both keys; if Claude prepends commentary including stray braces, parse may fail or capture extra.

Both 'Edit Plan' and 'Accept & Launch' currently invoke the same onPlanReady(generatedPlan) and close — there is no actual divergence between the two paths in this file; the parent must decide what mode to enter based on its own state.
getDashboardAdditionalContext is called synchronously to build additionalContextDirs — if localStorage is empty, returns [].
patterns: Provider-agnostic agent spawn: settings.agentProvider toggles between claude/codex, settings.{provider}CliPath supplies binary path. Same pattern in ProjectModal, ChatInstanceView, etc.
Tri-format stream-json parser (direct/NDJSON/result) — robust to streaming variants from Claude SDK, Claude CLI, and Codex.
useRef for listener-unsub functions and accumulated output, useState for reactive UI flags — splits mutable refs from rendered state correctly.
conventions: PlanPreview is an inner sub-component for the modal's preview block.
Planning prompt is a multi-line concatenation in buildPlanningPrompt — JSON schema is inlined verbatim.
